examples.ka: გლეხებს ძალიან უყვარდათ ამ ხის ქვეშ ბაასი
examples.roman: glexebs ʒalian uq̇vardat am xis kveš baasi
examples.en: Peasants really loved talking under this tree

words.en: angry, rambunctious, shirty, angered, incensed, riled
glosses: angry, rambunctious, shirty, angered, incensed, riled

words.en: amazingly, marvellously, miraculously, startlingly, strikingly, stupendously, surprisedly, surprisingly, wonderfully
glosses: amazingly, marvellously, miraculously, startlingly, strikingly, stupendously, surprisedly, surprisingly, wonderfully

words.en: courteous, pleasant, calm, well-bred (said especially about old people)
glosses: courteous, pleasant, calm, well-bred (said especially about old people)
words.etymology: Shanidze explains, that the word comes from დარბაზი (darbazi) + ერი (eri) through loss of ზ (z) between vowels and რ (r) dissimilation.

words.en: A dominant female in sadomasistic practices
glosses: A dominant female in sadomasistic practices

words.etymology: Inherited from Middle Georgian ელჩი (elči). Of Turkic-Mongolic origin, entering the Georgian language after the Mongol conquest (13th century). Compare Turkish elçi, Kazakh елші (elşı), Mongolian элч (elč).

From Old Georgian ვაჲ (vay). Akin to Mingrelian ვოი (voi), Laz ვოი (voi), Svan უ̂ა̈ჲ (ûäy). Probably natural exclamation. Compare also Persian وای (vây), Proto-Indo-European *wáy.
The adverbial usage is a semantic loan from Russian го́ре (góre).
From late Middle Georgian თათმანი (tatmani), borrowed from the Middle Armenian antecedent of dialectal Armenian թաթման (tʻatʻman). See թաթպան (tʻatʻpan) for more.
From Old Georgian იმედი (imedi), borrowed from the Late Middle Persian or Early New Persian descendant of Proto-Iranian *hamwátiš.
Inherited from Old Georgian კაზმვა (ḳazmva), most likely borrowed from Old Armenian կազմեմ (kazmem), from կազմ (kazm).

words.en: happy, cheerful, full of life, energetic, vigorous
glosses: happy, cheerful, full of life, energetic, vigorous

words.en: a person who is in love, enamoured
glosses: a person who is in love, enamoured

words.en: loving, showing a lot of love and care
glosses: loving, showing a lot of love and care

words.en: abduction of a woman in attempt to make her love the abductor; forced marriage
glosses: abduction of a woman in attempt to make her love the abductor; forced marriage

words.en: gloomy, somber, sad (of a person's face or mood)
glosses: gloomy, somber, sad (of a person's face or mood)
words.etymology: მო- (mo-) + ღრუბლ (ɣrubl, “cloud”) + -ული (-uli). A participle formation derived from the root ღრუბელი (ɣrubeli, “cloud”). The root undergoes syncopation (loss of the vowel ე (e)) when the suffix is added.

words.en: calm, tranquil, having a pleasant ambiance
glosses: calm, tranquil, having a pleasant ambiance

words.etymology: From Old Georgian მშჳდი (mšwidi). Akin to Mingrelian შვიდი (švidi).
From Old Georgian ნავღელი (navɣeli), further etymology is unknown. Cognate with Mingrelian ნაღმელი (naɣmeli) and Svan ნანღველ (nanɣvel).

words.en: A person or thing that is a source of hope
glosses: A person or thing that is a source of hope
words.etymology: From the prohibitive ნუ გეშინის (nu gešinis, “do not fear!”). For a possible parallel see Old Armenian մխիթար (mxitʻar).
From Persian رقیب (raqib), from Arabic رَقِيب (raqīb).

From Middle Georgian საბუჴარი (sabuqari), from the name of the city Bukhara. Compare Armenian բուխարա (buxara), բուխարի (buxari).
Cannot be related to ბუხარი (buxari), since it appeared in the language with ხ (x), not ჴ (q).

examples.ka: ე, მაგან ისრეთი სასირცხო საქმე ჩაიდინა რო!
examples.roman: e, magan isreti sasircxo sakme čaidina ro!

words.etymology: From Old Georgian საყუარელი (saq̇uareli), from Proto-Kartvelian *q̇war-.

words.en: odious, reviled, hatable, evoking hate, despicable, detestable
glosses: odious, reviled, hatable, evoking hate, despicable, detestable

words.etymology: From ამაყი (amaq̇i, “proud”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From ლამაზი (lamazi, “beautiful”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From მხიარული (mxiaruli, “cheerful”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From Old Georgian სირცხჳლი (sircxwili), most probably an archaic verbal noun.

words.en: abundance, affluence, generosity, opulence, over-abundance, plenty, profuseness, profusion, spillover
glosses: abundance, affluence, generosity, opulence, over-abundance, plenty, profuseness, profusion, spillover
words.etymology: From უხვი (uxvi, “abundant”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From Old Georgian სიყუარული (siq̇uaruli), from Proto-Kartvelian *q̇war-.

words.en: calm, hush, quiet, quietness, quietude, still, stillness, stills
glosses: calm, hush, quiet, quietness, quietude, still, stillness, stills
words.etymology: From ჩუმი (čumi, “quiet”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From წყნარი (c̣q̇nari, “calm”) + სი- -ე (si- -e, abstract noun-forming circumfix).
From Old Georgian სიხარული (sixaruli, “joy, happiness”), originally a verbal noun.

words.en: full-fledged, enjoying full rights of (such as membership)
glosses: full-fledged, enjoying full rights of (such as membership)

words.en: beloved (mostly used in vocative case as a way to address a woman)
glosses: beloved (mostly used in vocative case as a way to address a woman)
words.etymology: From Old Georgian ტურფაჲ (ṭurpay, “foreign (to one's eyes); beautiful”), from the same Iranian source as տարփ (tarpʻ). See the latter for more.

words.en: adversity, affliction, bale, blow, calamity, distress, misadventure, misery, misfortune, unhappiness, woe, wretchedness
glosses: adversity, affliction, bale, blow, calamity, distress, misadventure, misery, misfortune, unhappiness, woe, wretchedness

words.en: calamitous, hapless, ill-fated, sorry, unhappy, unlucky, unsuccessful, wretched, wretch
glosses: calamitous, hapless, ill-fated, sorry, unhappy, unlucky, unsuccessful, wretched, wretch
words.etymology: From ბედი (bedi, “fate, destiny, fortune”) + უ- -ური (u- -uri, circumfix for “un-“ or “without”).

words.en: happy-go-lucky, insouciant, light-hearted, thoughtless, unconcerned, unguarded
glosses: happy-go-lucky, insouciant, light-hearted, thoughtless, unconcerned, unguarded

words.en: being nagged, being disturbed, being in pain (due to bad conscience, worries, fears, doubts)
glosses: being nagged, being disturbed, being in pain (due to bad conscience, worries, fears, doubts)

words.en: malicious envy, destructive envy, hateful envy (literal meaning being black envy)
glosses: malicious envy, destructive envy, hateful envy (literal meaning being black envy)

words.en: foisting, saddling, or pawning off an unwanted object, tedious chore, or responsibility onto someone else
glosses: foisting, saddling, or pawning off an unwanted object, tedious chore, or responsibility onto someone else

examples.ka: კაი საქმე თავისთვის დაიტოვა და ეს მოსაწყენი დავალება მე შემტენა.
examples.roman: ḳai sakme tavistvis daiṭova da es mosac̣q̇eni davaleba me šemṭena.
examples.en: He kept the good project for himself and foisted this boring assignment onto me.

words.etymology: From the preverb შე- (še-, “into, inside”) + the root ტენ- (ṭen-, “to stuff, cram, pack”) + verbal noun suffix -ა (-a). Equivalent to the verbs შეტენის (šeṭenis), შეიტენის (šeiṭenis), შეუტენის (šeuṭenis), შეიტენება (šeiṭeneba), and შეეტენება (šeeṭeneba).
From past participle of შეიყვარებს (šeiq̇varebs) undergoing partial substantivization.
From Old Georgian შიში (šiši), further origin is unknown.

words.en: during the affect (excited, irritated etc. state)
glosses: during the affect (excited, irritated etc. state)

words.etymology: Of the same origin as წუხილი (c̣uxili).
From Old Georgian წუხილი (c̣uxili), from Proto-Kartvelian *c̣ux-. Cognate with Svan ლიწხუ̂ა̈უ̂ი (lic̣xûäûi).
